2011-05-01.title: Suicide Bomber Kills 8 at Iraqi Market

2011-05-01.abstract: Ollanta Humala, the front-runner in Peru’s coming presidential election, has distanced himself from the far-left leanings of his 2006 bid in favor of a more populist approach, but transforming voters’ doubts into support is proving difficult.

2011-05-01.title: Costly Afghanistan Road Project Is Marred by Unsavory Alliances
2011-05-01.abstract: The corruption surrounding a major American-financed highway has become another example of sinkholes in Afghanistan for the money of American taxpayers.

2011-05-01.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI moved his towering predecessor one step closer to sainthood in a celebratory Mass.

2011-05-01.title: Israel Holds Palestinian Funds as Deal Nears
2011-05-01.abstract: The Israeli Treasury said it would delay the transfer of $90 million in taxes collected on behalf of the Palestinian Authority.

2011-05-02.title: Life in Limbo for Japanese Near Nuclear Plant
2011-05-02.abstract: People outside evacuation zones are being exposed to radiation levels several times more than normal.

2011-05-02.abstract: A day after the Taliban declared the beginning of its spring offensive, the bombing in a town near the Pakistan border in southeastern Afghanistan killed 4 people and wounded 14.

2011-05-02.title: Cultivating Dinner
2011-05-02.abstract: Americans ate 475 million pounds of tilapia last year, making this once obscure African native the most popular farmed fish in the United States.

2011-05-02.title: Deal to End Yemen Crisis Is Faltering as Talks Bog
2011-05-02.abstract: Many in Yemen’s political opposition fear that President Ali Abdullah Saleh is deliberately stalling for time in an effort to outmaneuver his rivals.

2011-05-03.abstract: Federal officials said DNA analysis helped confirm Osama bin Laden’s identity with 99.9 percent accuracy, which scientific experts said would result from a match with a close relative.

2011-05-03.abstract: Desi Bouterse, a former military ruler who has been convicted of drug trafficking and is still on trial for official killings in the 1980s, is once again Suriname’s leader.

2011-05-03.title: Vatican: Bishop Who Broached New Rules for Priests Is Ousted
2011-05-03.abstract: The Vatican ousted an Australian bishop, William Morris, who had called on the church to consider ordaining women and married men.

2011-05-03.title: Property Lost in Holocaust Is Cataloged Online
2011-05-03.abstract: The database, listing more than half a million pieces of property, is part of a new project, financed by the Israeli government, to harness technology in the struggle for restitution.

2011-05-03.title: Conservatives in Canada Expand Party’s Hold
2011-05-03.abstract: The Conservative Party will again govern Canada, this time with a majority, following the country’s fourth election in seven years.

2011-05-04.title: Sudan: Deadly Clashes in Disputed Border Territory
2011-05-04.abstract: More than a dozen soldiers have been killed in clashes in Sudan’s contested Abyei area, Sudanese officials said on Tuesday.

2011-05-04.title: New U.S. Account Says Bin Laden Was Unarmed During Raid
2011-05-04.abstract: Osama bin Laden was not carrying a weapon when he was killed in Pakistan, the White House said, as it revised its initial account. Navy Seals shot him after a battle on the lower floors.

2011-05-04.abstract: A jury found a police officer’s “excessive and unreasonable” behavior during sometimes violent protests surrounding the Group of 20 meeting in 2009 led to the death of Ian Tomlinson.

2011-05-04.abstract: As questions mounted over how bin Laden hid in Pakistan, U.S. officials sought to avoid a break in relations.

2011-05-04.abstract: The raid that led to Bin Laden’s death has raised anew the issue of using torture to gain intelligence.

2011-05-05.title: Britain: 5 Freed Without Charges
2011-05-05.abstract: The police have released five men who were arrested this week near the Sellafield nuclear processing facility in northwest England.

2011-05-05.title: Canada: Bishop Pleads Guilty in Child Pornography Case
2011-05-05.abstract: A Roman Catholic bishop pleaded guilty in Ottawa on Wednesday to possession of child pornography for importation.

2011-05-05.title: Pakistani Army, Shaken by Raid, Faces New Scrutiny
2011-05-05.abstract: The raid that killed Osama bin Laden has provoked a crisis of confidence for what was long seen as the one institution that held Pakistan together.

2011-05-05.abstract: At an unusual news conference, a top Baghdad security official plays video confessions and suggests a connection to Al Qaeda.

2011-05-05.abstract: Members of Team 6, a secretive unit of men who endured years of brutal preparation, are the elite of the elite.

2011-05-06.abstract: Migrants continued to evacuate from Misurata, and a survivor of the shelling by Qaddafi forces on Wednesday returned to the tent camp where his children were killed.

2011-05-06.abstract: Bogotá’s hard-won accomplishments are being eclipsed by traffic chaos, attacks on tourists, and a corruption scandal that has resulted in the mayor’s suspension.

2011-05-06.abstract: Seized documents prompted U.S. officials to issue a warning that Al Qaeda had considered attacks on U.S. railroads. The C.I.A. also had Bin Laden’s compound under surveillance.

2011-05-06.abstract: A 2007 raid in Afghanistan that could have been former President George W. Bush’s last chance to capture or kill Osama Bin Laden did not materialize.

2011-05-06.title: Trapped in Another Country’s War

2011-05-07.abstract: Mr. Craig surged to international attention in the 1970s as the leader of the Ulster Vanguard, a right-wing umbrella group for Protestant partisans opposed to Irish nationalism.

2011-05-07.abstract: Mehrdad Oskouei has managed to make documentaries about female suicide, juvenile drug addiction and Iran’s crisis of youth identity without landing in a prison cell.

2011-05-08.abstract: An insurgent leader led an hours-long revolt inside a Baghdad jail, security authorities said.

2011-05-08.abstract: Japan remains committed to nuclear power despite the nuclear plant crisis, Prime Minister Naoto Kan said.

2011-05-20.abstract: The strikes expand the air campaign against what allied officials said was an increasing seaborne threat from Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi’s forces.

2011-05-27.title: Signs That Bin Laden Weighed Seeking Pakistani Protection
2011-05-27.abstract: Documents from the Abbottabad compound reveal discussions of promises of no Qaeda attacks in Pakistan in exchange for sheltering Bin Laden, but no evidence that an offer was made.

2011-05-28.title: Nigeria: 70 Suspected Militants Kill Six People in Multiple Attacks
2011-05-28.abstract: About 70 gunmen suspected of being Islamic militants attacked two police stations and a bank in the northeastern city of Maiduguri on Friday.

2011-05-28.title: A Day Off at the Monaco Grand Prix
2011-05-28.abstract: All sorts of little happenings here and there in the paddock in Monaco on the traditional down day of Friday.

2011-05-28.title: For First Time, Unicef Reveals Differences in Prices It Pays Drug Companies for Vaccines
2011-05-28.abstract: The decision to make the information public could lead to cuts in the agency’s costs.

2011-05-28.title: Arrest Follows Serbia’s Uneven Path to Break With Its Past
2011-05-28.abstract: For many Serbs, who are struggling with a very weak economy and high joblessness rates of some 20 percent, the arrest of Ratko Mladic is an ambivalent, even irrelevant moment for their near future.

2011-05-28.title: Israeli Troops Move Into Gaza
2011-05-28.abstract: Israel opens a ground war against Hamas after a week of intense airstrikes.

2011-05-29.title: In Russia, a Prison for the Police
2011-05-29.abstract: Correctional Colony 13 houses law enforcement officials who have committed abuses of power such as accepting bribes or beating detainees.

2011-05-29.title: In Russia, Prisons for Police Thrive
2011-05-29.abstract: Low pay for the police and other law enforcement officials drive them to corruption, analysts say.

2011-05-29.title: Retro Russian Import Lures Older Riders
2011-05-29.abstract: By the 1990s Irbit Motor Works seemed to be sputtering, but then it discovered a niche market in the United States for its sidecar motorcycles.

2011-05-29.title: Policy Adviser Tapped to Become U.S. Ambassador to Russia
2011-05-29.abstract: Michael McFaul, the architect of President Obama’s “reset policy” for Russia, has been named the next ambassador to that country.

2011-05-29.title: At Mission’s End — Joy, Relief and Unexpected Perils
2011-05-29.abstract: After a yearlong deployment to Afghanistan, one would think that going home would be the easiest thing troops could do. But the adjustment is not so simple.

2011-05-30.title: French Official Resigns Amid Harassment Suits
2011-05-30.abstract: Georges Tron quit as state secretary for civil service after two women filed complaints.

2011-05-30.title: An Ark Built for Today
2011-05-30.abstract: A man in Dordrecht, the Netherlands built an ark to teach others about the bible.

2011-05-30.title: A Biblical Blueprint Meets the Fire Code and the Neighbors
2011-05-30.abstract: A Dutch man’s vision of a modern-day ark may be more in tune with global warming and tourism than great floods, but it meets Noah’s specifications.

2011-05-30.title: Serb Fugitive Slowly Starved of Friends and Cash
2011-05-30.abstract: Critical questions remain about who protected war crimes fugitive Ratko Mladic and how such a wanted person managed to stay hidden for so long.

2011-05-30.title: Young and Bored, Behind the Wheel
2011-05-30.abstract: In Benghazi, with few outlets for rebellion, young people gather to watch “drifting,” a risky sport in which drivers purposely lose control of their vehicles.

2011-05-31.title: Sudan Border Strategy May Bring in Ethiopian Peacekeepers
2011-05-31.abstract: The contested status of an area straddling northern and southern Sudan has become a worrisome issue as the country prepares to split into two.

2011-05-31.title: Afghan Central Bank Defends Itself After Critical Report
2011-05-31.abstract: The governor of Afghanistan’s Central Bank said a report criticizing him and his agency for failing to uncover the fraud at a private bank was “completely biased” and “invalid.”

2011-05-31.title: 2 Americans Are Indicted in Iraq Contract Bribery
2011-05-31.abstract: An indictment says two American businessmen gave officers cash or plane tickets in exchange for multimillion-dollar military contracts.

2011-05-31.title: Abdias do Nascimento, Rights Voice, Dies at 97
2011-05-31.abstract: The Brazilian writer, painter, politician and scholar who was an outspoken civil rights leader on behalf of black Brazilians has died in Rio de Janeiro.

2011-05-31.title: A Wealth Gap in Equatorial Guinea
2011-05-31.abstract: While American companies have invested billions of dollars in this small nation’s oil infrastructure, World Bank statistics point to widespread poverty among much of the population.
